questions.question: Good governance means:
questions.solution:
Concept of Good Governance

Good governance is characterized by an administration that is transparent, accountable, and citizen-friendly. It ensures that the voices of the most vulnerable in society are heard in decision-making and that the government effectively meets the needs of the masses while following the rule of law.

questions.question: How many members are elected to the Public Accounts Committee from Rajya Sabha?
questions.solution:
Public Accounts Committee (PAC) Composition

The Public Accounts Committee consists of 22 members in total. Out of these, 15 members are elected from the Lok Sabha and 7 members are elected from the Rajya Sabha. The committee's primary role is to examine the audit reports of the CAG to ensure financial propriety by the government.

questions.question: Oaths and affirmations for various constitutional posts are provided in which Schedule of the Indian Constitution?
questions.solution:
Third Schedule: Oaths and Affirmations

The Third Schedule of the Indian Constitution contains the forms of oaths or affirmations for various constitutional offices, including Union Ministers, Members of Parliament, Judges of the Supreme Court and High Courts, and the Comptroller and Auditor General (CAG).

questions.question: The Attorney General of India is the highest law officer of the country appointed by whom?
questions.option_1: The seniormost Judge of Supreme Court
questions.solution:
Attorney General of India (Article 76)

The Attorney General of India is the highest law officer in the country. Under Article 76, they are appointed by the President of India. To be eligible, they must be qualified to be appointed as a Judge of the Supreme Court.

questions.question: The Directive Principles of State Policy in the Indian Constitution ____________.
questions.option_1: Are not enforceable by any court
questions.solution:
Enforceability of DPSPs

The Directive Principles of State Policy (DPSP) are not enforceable by any court (non-justiciable). This means that a citizen cannot move a court to force the government to implement these principles, although they are fundamental in the governance of the country and guide the State in making laws.

questions.question: Under the Right to Equality, of the Indian Constitution, which type of equality is ensured in public employment?
questions.solution:
Equality of Opportunity (Article 16)

Under the Right to Equality, Article 16 ensures equality of opportunity for all citizens in matters relating to public employment or appointment to any office under the State. It prohibits discrimination on grounds of religion, race, caste, sex, descent, place of birth, or residence.

questions.question: Where was the first Lokpal Day commemoration held in 2025?
questions.option_2: Zorawar Auditorium, Manekshaw Centre, Delhi Cantt
questions.solution:
First Lokpal Day (2025)

The first Lokpal Day commemoration, celebrating the establishment of the anti-corruption authority in India, was held in 2025 at the Zorawar Auditorium, Manekshaw Centre, Delhi Cantt. The Lokpal serves as the federal ombudsman to inquire into allegations of corruption against public functionaries.

questions.question: Which Article of the Indian Constitution directs the State to take steps to organize Village Panchayats?
questions.solution:
Article 40: Village Panchayats

Article 40 of the Directive Principles of State Policy directs the State to organize Village Panchayats and endow them with the necessary powers and authority to function as units of self-government. This laid the groundwork for the 73rd Amendment Act.

questions.question: Which of the following articles in the Indian Constitution states that there shall be a Council of Ministers with the Prime Minister at the head to aid and advise the President who shall, in the exercise of his functions, act in accordance with such advice?
questions.solution:
Article 74: Council of Ministers

Article 74 mandates that there shall be a Council of Ministers with the Prime Minister at the head to aid and advise the President. The 42nd and 44th Amendments further clarified that the President must act in accordance with such advice (though they may ask for a one-time reconsideration).

questions.question: Which of the following is a type of ‘urban local government’ according to the Constitution of India?
questions.solution:
Urban Local Government

According to the 74th Constitutional Amendment Act, a Municipality (which includes Municipal Corporations, Municipal Councils, and Nagar Panchayats) is the primary form of urban local government. Gram Sabha and Zila Panchayats are part of the rural local government system (Panchayati Raj).

questions.question: Which of the following is the correct method to change any provision of the Constitution of India?
questions.solution:
Methods of Constitutional Change

The only legitimate method to change any provision of the Indian Constitution is through a Constitutional Amendment under Article 368. This process requires varying levels of majorities in Parliament and, in cases affecting the federal structure, ratification by the States.

questions.question: Which of the following officially determines the constitutional validity of any legislation?
questions.option_1: The Supreme Court of India through judicial review
questions.option_2: The President of India through constitutional discretion
questions.option_3: The Union Cabinet through executive interpretation
questions.option_4: The Parliament of India through a special resolution
questions.solution:
Constitutional Validity and Judicial Review

The Supreme Court of India (and High Courts) officially determines the constitutional validity of any legislation through the power of judicial review. If a law is found to be in violation of the Constitution or its Basic Structure, the judiciary can declare it unconstitutional and void.
